name: GieliRogue
tagline: OSRS-themed browser roguelike built in React
narrative: GieliRogue is a browser-based roguelike that transplants Old School RuneScape's visual world into a turn-based dungeon crawler — zone navigation, inventory management, and monster encounters running entirely client-side in React + TypeScript.

Built a custom sprite pipeline: a TypeScript manifest script fetches and maps OSRS wiki assets at build time, keeping art sourcing reproducible without checking binaries into the repo. The entire game runs in the browser with no backend. Deployed to Vercel.

heroImage.alt: GieliRogue gameplay screenshot showing OSRS-themed dungeon crawler

name: DiTA
tagline: Digital maturity assessment with radar chart and PDF report
narrative: Built at a hackathon implementing the official DiTA working-group framework: a fully browser-based self-assessment that walks organisations through 24 questions across six dimensions — Organisation, Processes, Data, Technology, Strategy, and Implementation — returning a maturity score, per-dimension radar chart, and a downloadable PDF report for leadership review. No server, no account; all answers live in localStorage.

Chose Next.js 15 App Router for file-based routing across the sequential assessment flow, next-intl for bilingual DE/EN support with German as the authoritative content source, Zustand to carry state across pages, Recharts for the radar visualisation, and @react-pdf/renderer for the client-side PDF export. shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS kept the component layer fast to build while shipping a polished UI. Deployed to Vercel with analytics from day one.

heroImage.alt: DiTA assessment results showing radar chart and maturity scores across six dimensions

name: Konnektaro
narrative: Joined Konnektaro as Frontend Engineer to build a mobile-first progressive web app connecting learners and instructors through live sessions, asynchronous content, and a real-time networking graph.

Delivered the complete client from scratch: React + Next.js + TypeScript on the frontend, Express/Node.js API, Firebase for auth and hosting, MongoDB for persistence. Integrated OpenAI Whisper for live transcript generation. Shipped CI/CD on Firebase from day one.

heroImage.alt: Konnektaro app interface — Figma design preview

name: Tenant RES
narrative: Side project: a multi-tenant SaaS letting landlords manage properties, tenancies, maintenance issues, and rent ledgers — while tenants have a self-service portal for documents and requests.

Designed the full data model (ERD), REST API surface (OpenAPI spec), and auth layer (Firebase token verification with scoped roles) before writing a single line of product UI. The Figma flows cover landlord and tenant journeys end-to-end.

heroImage.alt: Tenant RES — landlord and tenant dashboard wireframes

artifacts.title: Database ERD (draw.io)
artifacts.description: Tables, relations, and how data is stored (tenancies, ledger, issues, docs).

artifacts.title: API + Auth design
artifacts.description: Firebase token verification, scoping rules, and endpoints (Swagger once running).

name: Fleet Manager
narrative: At iomoto (ZF Group) I took a fleet management platform from early conception through to public beta — a white-label SaaS securing real-time telemetry access for more than 5 000 vehicles across enterprise clients.

Led the frontend from scratch: React, strict WCAG-compliant component library, Azure AD SSO, Jest + Cypress for full test coverage. The white-label architecture let fleet operators customise branding without forking the codebase.

artifacts.title: Architecture (draw.io)
artifacts.description: Architecture of the Fleet Manager app.

name: Lity
tagline: B2C automotive sales app for electric vehicle discovery
narrative: At iomoto I joined the mobile team to build Lity — a Flutter app that helps drivers figure out whether an electric vehicle fits their life. Users connect real driving data, and the app runs it against EV specs to surface a fit score and direct comparisons against virtual EVs.
Owned the garage feature end-to-end and managed the login flow. Contributed across the main app and wrote unit tests throughout. Shipped to the Apple App Store.

name: This Portfolio
narrative: Built to be its own exhibit: a Vite + React + TypeScript SPA deployed to Firebase Hosting, styled with Tailwind v4 CSS-first tokens.

The Schematic visual direction — drafting-sheet white, Archivo Expanded headings, Sometype Mono data labels, hairline rules — treats the UI itself as an engineering artefact. Architecture diagram below.
